source.title: Lactate biosensors for spectrally and spatially multiplexed fluorescence imaging

gist_memory.paper_problem_memory: Previous extracellular L-lactate biosensor eLACCO1.1 had low ΔF/F (only 43% anionic chromophore in lactate-bound state), slow kinetics, and suboptimal membrane localization. Intracellular red biosensors were lacking or had poor performance. The goal was to develop improved green extracellular and red intracellular biosensors with higher ΔF/F, faster kinetics, and better localization for multiplexed imaging.

section_gists.heading: Development of a second-generation green fluorescent L-lactate biosensor, eLACCO2.1
section_gists.gist: Starting from eLACCO0.9 (faster than eLACCO1), directed evolution over 8 rounds (2 rounds random mutagenesis → eLACCO1.2, Cys340Ser → eLACCO1.3, 6 more rounds → eLACCO2) improved ΔF/F from 3.9 to 16. Affinity tuning via Leu79Ile produced eLACCO2.1 with Kd 1.9 mM and ΔF/F 14 (3.5× eLACCO1.1). Non-responsive control deLACCO1 created by Asp444Asn + 3 rounds brightness evolution.
process_signals: eLACCO0.9 chosen for faster kinetics over eLACCO1.
Cys340Ser introduced to avoid disulfide bond complications.
Affinity tuned because eLACCO2 Kd (280 µM) too low for extracellular mM range.
Leu79Ile selected from 10 variants for best balance of affinity and ΔF/F.
methods: Directed evolution by error-prone PCR of whole gene.
Screening of crude protein extracts for ΔF/F in 96-well plates.
Site-directed mutagenesis for affinity tuning and control biosensor.
failures_or_limits: eLACCO2 affinity too high for extracellular use.
Some variants in affinity tuning likely had lower ΔF/F.

section_gists.heading: Development of a red fluorescent biosensor for intracellular L-lactate, R-iLACCO1
section_gists.gist: R-iLACCO1 built by inserting cpmApple (from R-eLACCO1) into LldR at position 109. Initial R-iLACCO0.1 dim with ΔF/F 0.7. Linker optimization (2 aa deletion in C-terminal linker) improved brightness (R-iLACCO0.2, ΔF/F 0.6). 11 rounds of evolution (1 linker + 10 random mutagenesis) yielded R-iLACCO1 with ΔF/F 20. Lower affinity variants R-iLACCO1.1 and 1.2 identified by screening at multiple lactate concentrations. Control R-diLACCO1 (Asp69Asn + 2 rounds brightness).
process_signals: cpmApple chosen over TTHA0766-based RFP to avoid Ca2+ dependence.
Insertion site Leu109/Val110 in LldR.
Linker lengths critical for brightness.
Screening at 0, 0.5, 10 mM lactate to find lower affinity variants.
methods: Directed evolution: site-directed random mutagenesis or error-prone PCR.
Screening in E. coli crude extracts.
failures_or_limits: Initial linker variants may have had poor performance.

section_gists.heading: Optimization of cell surface localization
section_gists.gist: Screened 23 leaders and 10 anchors with eLACCO1.1. NGR GPI anchor gave best localization and higher ΔF/F vs CD59. HA leader with NGR anchor gave best signal in neurites. Final construct: HA-eLACCO2.1-NGR.
process_signals: Previous work showed leader/anchor combination critical.
Screening done in primary neurons.
CD59-eLACCO1.1-CD59 had puncta and lower ΔF/F.
methods: Fluorescence imaging to assess localization efficiency.
ΔF/F measurement in presence of lactate.
failures_or_limits: Many leader/anchor combinations likely gave poor localization or low response.

section_gists.heading: Characterization of eLACCO2.1 in live mammalian cells and acute brain slices
section_gists.gist: eLACCO2.1 showed good membrane localization, ΔF/F 8.8 in HeLa (138% of eLACCO1.1), faster kinetics (τon 57 s, τoff 20 s), photostability comparable to EGFP. In neurons, ΔF/F 8.1 (5.4× eLACCO1.1). In astrocytes, ΔF/F 7.3. In brain slices, detected endogenous lactate changes: decrease upon aglycemia, increase upon theta burst stimulation.
process_signals: eLACCO2.1 outperforms eLACCO1.1 in all metrics.
Ca2+ dependence noted but Kd (270 µM) below physiological extracellular Ca2+.
deLACCO1 control shows no response.
methods: HeLa cell imaging with lactate perfusion.
AAV transduction in mouse cortex, acute slice imaging, two-photon microscopy.
failures_or_limits: Ca2+ sensitivity could cause artifacts if extracellular Ca2+ drops below ~1 mM.

section_gists.heading: Characterization of R-iLACCO1 in live mammalian cells
section_gists.gist: R-iLACCO1 variants compared with existing sensors. R-iLACCO1.2 had highest ΔF/F in HeLa (11.2) and neurons (0.31). All R-iLACCO variants showed uniform localization, unlike some competitors. Table 3 summarizes performance.
process_signals: R-iLACCO1.2 chosen for multiplexing due to lower affinity and high ΔF/F.
Competitors (CanlonicSF, Green Lindoblum, GEM-IL, FiLa, Laconic) had lower ΔF/F or punctate localization.
methods: Expression in HeLa, HEK293T, neurons, astrocytes.
ΔF/F measurement with lactate or MCT inhibitor treatment.
failures_or_limits: R-iLACCO1.1 and 1.2 have lower ΔF/F than R-iLACCO1 in purified form but better in cells due to affinity matching.

section_gists.heading: In vivo imaging of eLACCO2.1 and R-iLACCO1.1 in mice
section_gists.gist: eLACCO2.1 responded to i.c.v. lactate injection in awake mice; deLACCO1 did not. eLACCO2.1 detected endogenous lactate decrease upon insulin i.p. in freely moving mice. R-iLACCO1.1 detected lactate increase in somatosensory cortex upon whisker stimulation.
process_signals: eLACCO2.1 functional in vivo.
R-iLACCO1.1 used for intracellular in vivo imaging.
methods: AAV expression in visual or somatosensory cortex.
Fiber photometry in freely moving mice.
failures_or_limits: No major failures reported, but in vivo signals may be small.

section_gists.heading: Spectrally and spatially multiplexed L-lactate imaging in glioblastoma cells
section_gists.gist: Coexpressed eLACCO2.1 and R-iLACCO1.2 in glioblastoma cells. Glucose stimulation increased both signals; intracellular signal showed transient peak coinciding with slower extracellular rise, suggesting MCT-mediated export. Also targeted R-iLACCO1.2 to mitochondria and ER, showing lactate shuttling to these organelles.
process_signals: Multiplexing enabled by spectral separation (green/red).
Transient peak in cytosol not seen with MCT inhibitor.
Organelle targeting demonstrates versatility.
failures_or_limits: No major failures, but interpretation of transient peak relies on inhibitor experiment.

section_gists.heading: Discussion
section_gists.gist: eLACCO2.1 improved by increasing anionic chromophore fraction (76% vs 43% in eLACCO1.1). Leader/anchor optimization critical. eLACCO2.1 outperforms eLACCO1.1 in localization, ΔF/F, kinetics. Ca2+ sensitivity may cause artifacts if extracellular Ca2+ drops to ~1 mM. Red biosensors enable multiplexing; R-iLACCO1 series outperforms existing sensors. Future work: further improve red sensor brightness and develop sensors for other metabolites.
process_signals: Rationale for directed evolution: increase anionic chromophore.
Acknowledgment of Ca2+ limitation.
Comparison table shows R-iLACCO1.2 best overall.
failures_or_limits: Ca2+ interference possible during extreme neural activity.
Red sensor brightness still lower than green.
Photostability of eLACCO2.1 lower in lactate-bound state.

section_gists.heading: General methods and materials
section_gists.gist: Standard molecular biology reagents and sources for genes (LldR, CanlonicSF, etc.).

section_gists.heading: Engineering of eLACCO2.1 and R-iLACCO1
section_gists.gist: Detailed directed evolution protocols: eLACCO2.1 from eLACCO0.9 via 8 rounds (2→eLACCO1.2, C340S→eLACCO1.3, 6→eLACCO2, L79I→eLACCO2.1). R-iLACCO1: cpmApple inserted into LldR, linker optimization, 11 rounds (1 linker + 10 random) → R-iLACCO1, with affinity variants from final round screening at multiple lactate concentrations. Control biosensors made by binding site mutation + brightness evolution.
process_signals: Library size: 200-400 colonies per round.
Screening in 96-well plates with plate reader.
Error-prone PCR for random mutagenesis.
Site-directed mutagenesis for specific changes.
failures_or_limits: No explicit failures, but many rounds needed.

global_hypotheses_about_storyline: Improving the fraction of anionic chromophore in the lactate-bound state is key to increasing ΔF/F for green sensors.
Using LldR instead of TTHA0766 avoids Ca2+ dependence for intracellular red sensors.
Optimizing leader and anchor sequences is essential for efficient membrane targeting and sensor performance in neurons.
Lower affinity variants are needed to match physiological lactate ranges and avoid saturation.
Spectral orthogonality (green/red) enables simultaneous extracellular and intracellular lactate imaging.
uncertainties_to_resolve: What were the specific mutations in eLACCO2 and R-iLACCO1, and how did they affect chromophore environment?
Were there any intermediate variants with poor performance that guided evolution?
How was the screening assay calibrated to avoid false positives?
What is the exact mechanism of Ca2+ sensitivity in eLACCO2.1?
Can the red sensor brightness be further improved for deeper tissue imaging?

events.summary: Previous extracellular L-lactate biosensor eLACCO1.1 suffers from aggregation and limited fluorescence response on mammalian cell surface.
No red fluorescent biosensors for intracellular L-lactate have been reported, hindering multiplexed imaging.
Designed a spectrally and functionally orthogonal pair: green extracellular eLACCO2.1 and red intracellular R-iLACCO1.
R-iLACCO1 excitation and emission spectra measured with and without 10 mM L-lactate.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk introduces the problem with previous sensors, the design of new orthogonal pair, and initial characterization data.

events.summary: eLACCO0.9 chosen as starting point for directed evolution due to faster kinetics than eLACCO1.
Linker optimization and 11 rounds of directed evolution improved R-iLACCO0.1 to R-iLACCO1 with ΔF/F of 20.
Screening of 23 leaders and 10 anchors identified HA leader and NGR anchor as optimal for cell surface localization and ΔF/F.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk covers directed evolution of eLACCO2.1 and R-iLACCO1, affinity tuning, and leader/anchor optimization. All events are process-oriented and supported by quotes.
Chunk describes optimization of cell surface localization by screening leader/anchor combinations, identifying best performers, and noting a failure with CD59 anchor.

events.summary: R-iLACCO variants functional in primary neurons and astrocytes, showing robust response to MCT inhibition.
methods_or_tools: fluorescence imaging of primary neurons and astrocytes

chunk_events.notes: Chunk covers characterization of R-iLACCO variants in HeLa cells, including response magnitude, affinity, kinetics, photoactivation, photostability, and performance in glucose stimulation and MCT inhibition, plus validation in neurons and astrocytes.

events.summary: In situ L-lactate titration in HeLa cells reveals apparent Kd values for R-iLACCO variants.
Comparison of intracellular L-lactate biosensors shows R-iLACCO1.2 has best performance in HeLa, HEK293T, neurons, and astrocytes.
eLACCO2.1 has 76% anionic chromophore in lactate-bound state, improving ΔF/F over eLACCO1.1 (43%).

chunk_events.notes: Chunk contains characterization data for R-iLACCO variants, comparison table, discussion of eLACCO2.1 improvements and limitations, and validation of red sensor performance.

events.summary: R-iLACCO1 and affinity variants display no undesirable punctate intracellular accumulation.
R-iLACCO1 variants are best-in-class intracellular L-lactate biosensors for many cell-based applications.
Multiplexed imaging with R-iLACCO1.2 and eLACCO2.1 or iLACCO1 enabled monitoring L-lactate dynamics in various cell compartments.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk discusses limitations of red biosensors, directed evolution to improve R-iLACCO1, validation of its performance, analysis of Kd discrepancy, and multiplexed imaging applications.

events.summary: Spectrally and spatially multiplexed L-lactate imaging in glioblastoma cells using eLACCO2.1 and R-iLACCO1.2.
Glucose stimulation increased both extracellular and intracellular lactate signals, with a transient intracellular peak coinciding with slower extracellular rise.
Targeted R-iLACCO1.2 to mitochondrial matrix using COXIV targeting sequence for organelle-specific lactate imaging.
Glucose stimulation caused a rapid increase in mitochondrial lactate signal, followed by a decline, while cytosolic signal remained elevated.
Targeted R-iLACCO1.2 to endoplasmic reticulum using calreticulin targeting sequence and KDEL retention signal.
Glucose stimulation increased lactate signal in both cytosol and ER, with similar kinetics.
Engineering of eLACCO2.1 involved iterative directed evolution from eLACCO0.9, including Cys340Ser mutation and Leu79Ile affinity tuning.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk contains multiplexed imaging experiments and detailed engineering methods for eLACCO2.1 and R-iLACCO1.

events.summary: Linker optimization of R-iLACCO0.1 by deleting N- and C-terminal linkers to improve brightness and response.
Directed evolution of R-iLACCO0.2 through 11 rounds of library generation and screening to yield R-iLACCO1, R-iLACCO1.1, and R-iLACCO1.2.
Control biosensor R-diLACCO1 created by Asp69Asn mutation to abrogate lactate affinity, followed by two rounds of brightness evolution.
In vitro characterization of eLACCO2.1 and R-iLACCO variants including pH titrations, lactate titrations, and Ca2+ titrations.
Two-photon excitation spectra and cross sections measured for eLACCO2.1 and R-iLACCO1 to characterize chromophore forms.

chunk_events.notes: This chunk contains detailed methods for biosensor engineering, protein characterization, and live-cell imaging, directly supporting the paper's process of developing and testing lactate biosensors.

events.summary: Two-photon imaging of eLACCO2.1 in acute cortical brain slices to detect endogenous lactate changes during aglycemia and electrical stimulation.
In vivo validation of eLACCO2.1 by fiber photometry in awake mice with i.c.v. lactate injection.
In vivo eLACCO2.1 imaging in freely moving mice to detect endogenous lactate decrease upon insulin i.p. injection.
In vivo R-iLACCO imaging in anesthetized mice to detect lactate increase in somatosensory cortex upon whisker stimulation.
numbers_or_metrics: 50 ms, 20 psi, 5 Hz air puffs for 30 s

events.summary: Statistical analysis using two-tailed Student's t test or one-way ANOVA with Dunnett's post hoc tests.
eLACCO2.1 detected endogenous lactate changes in brain slices: decrease upon aglycemia, increase upon theta burst stimulation.
eLACCO2.1 responded to i.c.v. lactate injection in awake mice; deLACCO1 control did not.

chunk_events.notes: This chunk describes in vivo and ex vivo validation experiments for eLACCO2.1 and R-iLACCO, including methods, analysis, and validation events.
This chunk contains only references, acknowledgements, author contributions, competing interests, and additional information. No research evolution events are described.

Leu79Ile mutation was added to eLACCO2 to tune the L-lactate affinity. The resulting mutant was designated as eLACCO2.1.

N- and C-terminal linkers of R-iLACCO0.1 were deleted using Q5 high-fidelity DNA polymerase (New England Biolabs) to provide variants with different linker length. Variants were expressed in E. coli. Proteins were extracted using B-PER bacterial protein extraction reagent (Thermo Fisher Scientific) and tested for fluorescence brightness and L-lactate-dependent response. The most promising variant, designated as R-iLACCO0.2

R-iLACCO0.2, was subjected to an iterative process of library generation and screening in E. coli. Libraries were generated by site-directed random mutagenesis or error-prone PCR of the whole gene. For each round, approximately 200–400 fluorescent colonies were picked, cultured, and tested on 96-well plates under a plate reader. There were 11 rounds of screening before R-iLACCO1, R-iLACCO1.1, and R-iLACCO1.2 were identified.

Asp69Asn mutation was added to R-iLACCO1 to abrogate the L-lactate affinity. Two rounds of directed evolution were performed to improve the brightness. The resulting control mutant was designated as R-diLACCO1.

To perform pH titrations for eLACCO2.1, protein solutions were diluted into buffers (pH from 4 to 11) containing 30 mM trisodium citrate, 30 mM sodium borate, 30 mM MOPS, 100 mM KCl, 1 mM CaCl₂, and either no L-lactate or 100 mM L-lactate.
Two-photon excitation spectra and two-photon absorption cross sections of eLACCO2.1 and R-iLACCO1 were measured according to the general methods and protocols described in ref. 44.
Rhodamine 6 G in methanol was used as a reference standard with excitation at 1060 nm (ref. 44).

event_graph.paper_main_problem: Develop improved green extracellular and red intracellular L-lactate biosensors with higher ΔF/F, faster kinetics, and better localization for multiplexed imaging.

canonical_events.summary: eLACCO1.1 suffers from aggregation and limited fluorescence response on mammalian cell surface.
No red fluorescent biosensors for intracellular L-lactate exist, hindering multiplexed imaging.
Design orthogonal pair: green extracellular eLACCO2.1 and red intracellular R-iLACCO1.
Choose eLACCO0.9 as starting point for green sensor evolution due to faster kinetics than eLACCO1.
Directed evolution of eLACCO0.9: 2 rounds random mutagenesis → eLACCO1.2, Cys340Ser → eLACCO1.3, 6 more rounds → eLACCO2 (ΔF/F 16).
Affinity tuning of eLACCO2 via Leu79Ile to produce eLACCO2.1 (Kd 1.9 mM, ΔF/F 14).
Create non-responsive control deLACCO1 by Asp444Asn mutation + 3 rounds brightness evolution.
Construct R-iLACCO0.1 by inserting cpmApple into LldR at position 109; initial ΔF/F 0.7, dim.
Linker optimization of R-iLACCO0.1: 2 aa deletion in C-terminal linker → R-iLACCO0.2 (ΔF/F 0.6).
11 rounds of directed evolution (1 linker + 10 random) → R-iLACCO1 (ΔF/F 20).
Screen final round at multiple lactate concentrations to identify lower affinity variants R-iLACCO1.1 and 1.2.
Create control R-diLACCO1 by Asp69Asn mutation + 2 rounds brightness evolution.
Screen 23 leaders and 10 anchors; HA leader + NGR anchor optimal for eLACCO2.1 surface localization and ΔF/F.
eLACCO2.1 in HeLa: ΔF/F 8.8 (138% of eLACCO1.1), faster kinetics (τon 57 s, τoff 20 s), photostability comparable to EGFP.
eLACCO2.1 detects endogenous lactate changes in brain slices: decrease upon aglycemia, increase upon theta burst stimulation.
R-iLACCO1.2 shows best performance in HeLa (ΔF/F 11.2), HEK293T, neurons, astrocytes; uniform localization.
R-iLACCO variants functional in primary neurons and astrocytes; robust response to MCT inhibition.
In vivo: eLACCO2.1 responds to i.c.v. lactate injection in awake mice; deLACCO1 does not.

Lack of red intracellular sensor motivates design of orthogonal pair.
Design leads to selection of starting point for green sensor evolution.

eLACCO2 affinity tuned to produce eLACCO2.1.
Control biosensor deLACCO1 created from eLACCO2.
Design of red sensor begins with construction of R-iLACCO0.1.
Linker optimization improves R-iLACCO0.1 to R-iLACCO0.2.
Screening identifies lower affinity variants R-iLACCO1.1 and 1.2.
eLACCO2.1 requires optimal leader/anchor for surface localization.
Optimized localization allows characterization in HeLa cells.
R-iLACCO1.2 selected for best performance in mammalian cells.
Validation in primary neurons and astrocytes.
In vivo validation of eLACCO2.1 with lactate injection.

must_cover_points: Problem with eLACCO1.1 and lack of red intracellular sensor
